mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly declines below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling economic deceleration that reduces corporate earnings expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value=20182.491 with high revision risk
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of periods with GDPC1 growth below 1.5%, or correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and forward returns drops below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase preceding recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances despite UNRATE rising > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline simultaneously by more than 5% month-over-month, indicating synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand that signals imminent economic weakness and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5% MoM AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM in same month
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with synchronized weakness across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances (both RSAFS and HOUST down > 5%), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines for 2+ consecutive months while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3% year-over-year, indicating stagflationary conditions that compress profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines 2+ consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with supply constraints and persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered stagflation instances, or joint effect of INDPRO decline and high inflation is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in interaction model over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50,000 jobs per month for 2+ consecutive months while unemployment remains below 4%, indicating labor market softening despite tight conditions that signals peak employment cycle and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth < 50k jobs/month for 2+ months AND UNRATE < 4%
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market with decelerating job growth despite tight unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction between PAYEMS deceleration and low UNRATE does not significantly predict negative returns (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 4%, signaling disinflation that creates expectations for future rate cuts and reduces real borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: disinflation phase within restrictive monetary cycle creating rate-cut expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered disinflation instances, or the interaction effect between CPIAUCSL decline and elevated FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) increase by more than 10% month-over-month for 2+ consecutive months, signaling robust residential investment and forward-looking economic optimism that drives positive equity returns through earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases > 10% MoM for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value=1450.0 with medium revision risk
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or early-cycle expansion with housing-led demand acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered housing acceleration instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during housing expansion periods.
